intents.name: service.restart
intents.description: Restart a service in an environment
intents.command: sudo systemctl restart {{service}} && sudo systemctl status {{service}} --no-pager

intents.name: service.status
intents.description: Check the status of a service
intents.commandWindows: powershell -Command "$svc = Get-Service {{service}} -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue; if($svc) { $svc | Format-List Name,Status,StartType } else { $proc = Get-Process -Name '*{{service}}*' -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue; if($proc) { $proc | Format-Table Id,ProcessName,CPU,WorkingSet -AutoSize; Write-Host 'Process is running (not a Windows service)' } else { Write-Host '{{service}} is NOT running' } }"

intents.name: service.list
intents.description: List running services

intents.name: server.check_disk
intents.description: Check disk usage on a target server or specific path
intents.command: df -h && echo '\nTop directories:\n' && du -sh /var/* 2>/dev/null | sort -rh | head -10
examples: show disk space on api server
how much space on my documents
what is eating space on staging
intents.commandWindows: powershell -Command "Write-Host 'Filesystem      Size  Used Avail Use% Mounted on'; Get-CimInstance Win32_LogicalDisk -Filter 'DriveType=3' | ForEach-Object { $s=[math]::Round($_.Size/1GB,1); $f=[math]::Round($_.FreeSpace/1GB,1); $u=[math]::Round(($_.Size-$_.FreeSpace)/1GB,1); $p=if($_.Size -gt 0){[math]::Round(($_.Size-$_.FreeSpace)/$_.Size*100)}else{0}; '{0,-15} {1}G {2}G {3}G {4}% {5}' -f $_.DeviceID,$s,$u,$f,$p,($_.DeviceID+'\') }"

intents.name: server.check_memory
intents.description: Check memory usage on a target server
intents.command:
free -h && echo '
Top 15 processes by memory:
' && ps aux --sort=-%mem | head -16 | awk '{printf "%-10s %-8s %-6s %-6s %s
", $1, $2, $4"%", $6/1024"MB", $11}'
examples: how much ram is used on staging

intents.name: server.uptime
intents.description: Check server uptime, load, and top processes with analysis
synonyms: how long has the server been running
how long has this been running
what is the load on my server
how long has server been up
intents.command: echo '=== Uptime ===' && uptime && echo '' && echo '=== CPUs ===' && nproc && echo '' && echo '=== Load ===' && cat /proc/loadavg 2>/dev/null && echo '' && echo '=== Memory ===' && free -h 2>/dev/null && echo '' && echo '=== Top Processes (by CPU) ===' && ps aux --sort=-%cpu | head -8 && echo '' && echo '=== Top Processes (by Memory) ===' && ps aux --sort=-%mem | head -6
examples: how long has prod been up
what is the load on my server
how long has the server been running

intents.name: logs.find
intents.description: Find and list log files on the system, optionally for a specific service
intents.command: echo '=== /var/log ===' && ls -lhS /var/log/*.log /var/log/*/*.log 2>/dev/null | head -20 && echo '' && echo '=== Large logs (>50MB) ===' && find /var/log -type f -size +50M -exec ls -lh {} \; 2>/dev/null && echo '' && echo '=== Recently modified ===' && find /var/log -type f -mmin -60 -name '*.log' 2>/dev/null | head -10
examples: where are the logs on staging
what logs are on the server
find logs for nginx on prod

intents.name: logs.errors
intents.description: Search for errors across common log files
intents.command: echo '=== Syslog errors ===' && grep -i 'error\|fail\|critical' /var/log/syslog 2>/dev/null | tail -10 || grep -i 'error\|fail\|critical' /var/log/messages 2>/dev/null | tail -10 && echo '' && echo '=== Auth errors ===' && grep -i 'error\|fail' /var/log/auth.log 2>/dev/null | tail -5 || grep -i 'error\|fail' /var/log/secure 2>/dev/null | tail -5 && echo '' && echo '=== Nginx errors ===' && tail -10 /var/log/nginx/error.log 2>/dev/null || echo 'No nginx error log' && echo '' && echo '=== Kernel errors ===' && dmesg --level=err,crit,alert,emerg 2>/dev/null | tail -5
examples: check logs for errors on prod

intents.name: logs.check
intents.description: Check a specific log file — shows recent entries, size, and rotation status
intents.command: echo '=== File info ===' && ls -lh {{path}} && echo '' && echo '=== Last {{lines}} lines ===' && tail -{{lines}} {{path}} && echo '' && echo '=== Rotation ===' && ls -lh {{path}}* 2>/dev/null | head -5
examples: check the nginx error log on staging

intents.name: logs.tail
intents.description: Tail logs for a service
examples: show me the last 100 lines for api logs

intents.name: logs.search
intents.description: Search logs for a pattern
examples: search for errors in api logs
find timeout in nginx logs on prod

intents.name: deploy.run
intents.description: Run a deployment
intents.command: cd /srv/myapp && git fetch origin && git checkout {{branch}} && git pull origin {{branch}} && ./deploy.sh

intents.name: deploy.rollback
intents.description: Rollback a deployment

intents.description: List running Docker containers

intents.description: Restart a Docker container

bounce the redis container in staging

intents.name: docker.stop
intents.description: Stop a running Docker container

intents.name: docker.start
intents.description: Start a stopped Docker container

intents.name: docker.logs
intents.description: View logs for a Docker container

intents.name: docker.all
intents.description: List all containers including stopped ones

intents.name: docker.images
intents.description: List Docker images

intents.name: docker.cleanup
intents.description: Clean up unused/orphaned Docker resources — stopped containers, dangling images, unused volumes and networks
intents.command: echo '=== Stopped containers ===' && docker ps -a --filter status=exited --format 'table {{.Names}}\t{{.Status}}\t{{.Size}}' && echo '' && echo '=== Dangling images ===' && docker images -f dangling=true --format 'table {{.Repository}}\t{{.Tag}}\t{{.Size}}' && echo '' && echo '=== Disk usage ===' && docker system df && echo '' && echo 'Run "docker system prune -a" to clean up (will ask for confirmation)'

intents.name: docker.prune
intents.description: Remove all stopped containers, unused images, networks, and volumes
intents.command: docker system prune -af --volumes && echo '' && docker system df

intents.name: docker.compose_up
intents.description: Start services with docker compose
examples: docker compose up api on prod

intents.name: docker.compose_down
intents.description: Stop and remove docker compose services

intents.name: docker.compose_build
intents.description: Build or rebuild docker compose services

intents.name: docker.compose_status
intents.description: Show status of docker compose services

intents.name: docker.compose_restart
intents.description: Restart docker compose services

intents.name: cron.list
intents.description: List all cron jobs — user crontab, system crons, and timers
intents.command: echo '=== User Crontab ===' && crontab -l 2>/dev/null || echo '(no user crontab)' && echo '' && echo '=== System Crons (/etc/cron.d/) ===' && ls -la /etc/cron.d/ 2>/dev/null || echo '(none)' && echo '' && echo '=== Cron Directories ===' && echo 'Hourly:' && ls /etc/cron.hourly/ 2>/dev/null || echo '  (empty)' && echo 'Daily:' && ls /etc/cron.daily/ 2>/dev/null || echo '  (empty)' && echo 'Weekly:' && ls /etc/cron.weekly/ 2>/dev/null || echo '  (empty)' && echo 'Monthly:' && ls /etc/cron.monthly/ 2>/dev/null || echo '  (empty)' && echo '' && echo '=== Systemd Timers ===' && systemctl list-timers --no-pager 2>/dev/null | head -20 || echo '(no systemd timers)'

intents.name: network.ports
intents.description: Show listening ports on a server
examples: what ports are listening on staging

intents.name: network.ping
intents.description: Ping a host or check connectivity

intents.name: files.find
intents.description: Find files or directories by name or pattern

intents.name: files.find_media
intents.description: Find media files — videos, images, music, documents
intents.command: echo '=== Media Files ===' && find {{path}} -maxdepth 4 -type f \( -iname '*.mp4' -o -iname '*.mkv' -o -iname '*.avi' -o -iname '*.mov' -o -iname '*.wmv' -o -iname '*.flv' -o -iname '*.webm' -o -iname '*.mp3' -o -iname '*.flac' -o -iname '*.wav' -o -iname '*.aac' -o -iname '*.ogg' -o -iname '*.m4a' -o -iname '*.jpg' -o -iname '*.jpeg' -o -iname '*.png' -o -iname '*.gif' -o -iname '*.bmp' -o -iname '*.webp' -o -iname '*.svg' -o -iname '*.tiff' -o -iname '*.pdf' -o -iname '*.doc' -o -iname '*.docx' -o -iname '*.ppt' -o -iname '*.pptx' -o -iname '*.xls' -o -iname '*.xlsx' \) -not -path '*/node_modules/*' -not -path '*/.git/*' 2>/dev/null | sort && echo '' && echo '=== Summary ===' && echo 'Videos:' && find {{path}} -maxdepth 4 -type f \( -iname '*.mp4' -o -iname '*.mkv' -o -iname '*.avi' -o -iname '*.mov' -o -iname '*.webm' \) -not -path '*/node_modules/*' 2>/dev/null | wc -l && echo 'Images:' && find {{path}} -maxdepth 4 -type f \( -iname '*.jpg' -o -iname '*.jpeg' -o -iname '*.png' -o -iname '*.gif' -o -iname '*.webp' -o -iname '*.svg' \) -not -path '*/node_modules/*' 2>/dev/null | wc -l && echo 'Audio:' && find {{path}} -maxdepth 4 -type f \( -iname '*.mp3' -o -iname '*.flac' -o -iname '*.wav' -o -iname '*.aac' -o -iname '*.ogg' \) -not -path '*/node_modules/*' 2>/dev/null | wc -l && echo 'Documents:' && find {{path}} -maxdepth 4 -type f \( -iname '*.pdf' -o -iname '*.doc' -o -iname '*.docx' -o -iname '*.ppt' -o -iname '*.pptx' -o -iname '*.xls' -o -iname '*.xlsx' \) -not -path '*/node_modules/*' 2>/dev/null | wc -l

intents.name: files.send
intents.description: Archive a folder and send it to a remote server
synonyms: zip up my project and send
zip up my project folder and send
intents.command: ARCHIVE=$(basename {{source}})-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S).tar.gz && tar -czf /tmp/$ARCHIVE -C $(dirname {{source}}) $(basename {{source}}) && echo "Created /tmp/$ARCHIVE ($(du -sh /tmp/$ARCHIVE | cut -f1))" && scp /tmp/$ARCHIVE {{environment}}:{{destination}}/$ARCHIVE && echo "Sent to {{environment}}:{{destination}}/$ARCHIVE" && rm /tmp/$ARCHIVE
examples: zip up my project folder and send it to prod /root
archive and send this folder to dev

intents.name: package.install
intents.description: Install a system package
intents.command: if command -v apt-get >/dev/null; then sudo apt-get install -y {{package}}; elif command -v dnf >/dev/null; then sudo dnf install -y {{package}}; elif command -v yum >/dev/null; then sudo yum install -y {{package}}; else echo 'No supported package manager found'; fi

intents.description: Check if a package is installed
intents.command: command -v {{package}} && echo '{{package}} is installed' && {{package}} --version 2>/dev/null || dpkg -l {{package}} 2>/dev/null || rpm -q {{package}} 2>/dev/null || echo '{{package}} is NOT installed'

intents.name: swap.create
intents.description: Create and enable a swap file
intents.command: sudo fallocate -l {{size}} {{path}} && sudo chmod 600 {{path}} && sudo mkswap {{path}} && sudo swapon {{path}} && echo '{{path}} ({{size}}) activated' && free -h

intents.name: swap.delete
intents.description: Disable and remove a swap file
intents.command: sudo swapoff {{path}} && sudo rm -f {{path}} && echo 'Swap removed: {{path}}' && free -h

intents.name: cert.generate
intents.description: Generate a self-signed SSL certificate
intents.command: sudo openssl req -x509 -nodes -days 365 -newkey rsa:2048 -keyout {{path}}/private/{{domain}}.key -out {{path}}/certs/{{domain}}.crt -subj '/CN={{domain}}' && echo 'Certificate generated for {{domain}}'

make me a picture of a mountain

intents.name: ai.image_status
intents.description: Check status of image generation engines — what we use to make images
synonyms: what are we using to generate
what are we using for images

intents.name: ai.install_sd
intents.description: Install Stable Diffusion or other image generation software

intents.name: ai.start_sd
intents.description: Start the Stable Diffusion web UI

intents.name: ai.stop_sd
intents.description: Stop the Stable Diffusion web UI
intents.command: pkill -f 'webui.py\|stable-diffusion' 2>/dev/null && echo 'Stable Diffusion stopped' || echo 'Not running'

intents.name: ai.gpu_mode
intents.description: Switch image generation between GPU and CPU mode

intents.name: hardware.gpu
intents.description: Show GPU information — model, VRAM, temperature, utilization

intents.name: dns.lookup
intents.description: Look up DNS records for a domain
intents.command: echo '=== DNS Records ===' && dig {{domain}} {{type}} +short && echo '' && echo '=== All Records ===' && dig {{domain}} ANY +noall +answer 2>/dev/null && echo '' && echo '=== Nameservers ===' && dig {{domain}} NS +short
examples: what are the dns records for notoken.sh

intents.name: dns.update
intents.description: Update a DNS record (via provider CLI or /etc/hosts)
intents.command: echo '{{domain}} → {{value}} ({{type}})' && echo 'To update /etc/hosts:' && echo '{{value}} {{domain}}' && echo '' && echo 'For cloud DNS, use your provider CLI:' && echo '  aws route53 change-resource-record-sets ...' && echo '  gcloud dns record-sets update ...' && echo '  doctl compute domain records create ...'
examples: add A record for api.example.com pointing to 1.2.3.4

intents.name: firewall.list
intents.description: Show firewall rules (ufw, iptables, firewalld)
intents.command: echo '=== UFW ===' && ufw status verbose 2>/dev/null || echo '(ufw not active)' && echo '' && echo '=== iptables ===' && iptables -L -n --line-numbers 2>/dev/null | head -30 || echo '(no iptables access)' && echo '' && echo '=== firewalld ===' && firewall-cmd --list-all 2>/dev/null || echo '(firewalld not active)'

intents.name: firewall.open
intents.description: Open a port in the firewall
intents.command: ufw allow {{port}}/{{protocol}} 2>/dev/null && ufw status || firewall-cmd --add-port={{port}}/{{protocol}} --permanent 2>/dev/null && firewall-cmd --reload || iptables -A INPUT -p {{protocol}} --dport {{port}} -j ACCEPT

intents.name: firewall.close
intents.description: Close/block a port in the firewall
intents.command: ufw deny {{port}}/{{protocol}} 2>/dev/null && ufw status || firewall-cmd --remove-port={{port}}/{{protocol}} --permanent 2>/dev/null && firewall-cmd --reload || iptables -A INPUT -p {{protocol}} --dport {{port}} -j DROP

intents.name: firewall.block_ip
intents.description: Block an IP address

intents.name: systemd.create
intents.description: Create a systemd service unit for an application
intents.command:
cat > /etc/systemd/system/{{name}}.service << 'UNIT'
[Unit]
Description={{name}} service
After=network.target

[Service]
Type=simple
User={{user}}
WorkingDirectory={{workdir}}
ExecStart={{command}}
Restart=on-failure
RestartSec=5

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
UNIT
systemctl daemon-reload && systemctl enable {{name}} && systemctl start {{name}} && echo 'Service created and started' && systemctl status {{name}} --no-pager
examples: create systemd service for my node app
make my api run on boot

intents.name: package.audit
intents.description: Check for outdated or vulnerable packages
intents.command: echo '=== Outdated Packages ===' && (npm outdated 2>/dev/null || pip list --outdated 2>/dev/null || echo '(no package manager detected)') && echo '' && echo '=== Security Audit ===' && (npm audit 2>/dev/null || pip-audit 2>/dev/null || echo '(no audit tool available)')

intents.name: package.update_all
intents.description: Update all packages to latest versions
intents.command: if [ -f package.json ]; then npm update && echo 'npm packages updated'; elif [ -f requirements.txt ]; then pip install --upgrade -r requirements.txt && echo 'pip packages updated'; elif [ -f Gemfile ]; then bundle update && echo 'gems updated'; else echo 'No package manager detected'; fi

intents.name: runtime.install
intents.description: Install or switch to a specific runtime version
intents.command: echo 'Installing {{runtime}} {{version}}...' && if echo '{{runtime}}' | grep -qi 'node'; then nvm install {{version}} 2>/dev/null && nvm use {{version}} || echo 'Install nvm first: notoken fix nvm'; elif echo '{{runtime}}' | grep -qi 'python'; then pyenv install {{version}} 2>/dev/null && pyenv global {{version}} || apt-get install -y python{{version}} 2>/dev/null || echo 'Use pyenv or apt'; elif echo '{{runtime}}' | grep -qi 'go'; then curl -sSL https://go.dev/dl/go{{version}}.linux-amd64.tar.gz | tar -C /usr/local -xz; elif echo '{{runtime}}' | grep -qi 'rust'; then curl --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 -sSf https://sh.rustup.rs | sh -s -- -y; else echo 'Unknown runtime: {{runtime}}'; fi

intents.name: cloud.aws
intents.description: Run AWS CLI commands

intents.name: cloud.gcloud
intents.description: Run Google Cloud CLI commands

intents.name: cloud.az
intents.description: Run Azure CLI commands

intents.name: cloud.do
intents.description: Run DigitalOcean CLI commands

intents.name: monitor.watch_log
intents.description: Watch a log file in real-time with optional filter
intents.command: echo 'Watching {{path}}...' && if [ -n '{{filter}}' ] && [ '{{filter}}' != '{{filter}}' ]; then tail -f {{path}} | grep --line-buffered '{{filter}}'; else tail -f {{path}}; fi

intents.name: monitor.disk_alert
intents.description: Check if disk usage exceeds a threshold
intents.command: echo '=== Disk Usage Check (threshold: {{threshold}}%) ===' && df -h --output=target,pcent,size,avail | tail -n +2 | while read mount pct size avail; do num=$(echo $pct | tr -d '%'); if [ "$num" -ge {{threshold}} ] 2>/dev/null; then echo "⚠ $mount: $pct used ($avail free of $size)"; fi; done && echo '' && echo '=== All mounts ===' && df -h | head -15

intents.name: monitor.process
intents.description: Monitor a specific process — CPU, memory, uptime
intents.command: echo '=== Top by CPU ===' && ps aux --sort=-%cpu | head -10 && echo '' && echo '=== Top by Memory ===' && ps aux --sort=-%mem | head -10 && echo '' && echo '=== Process Tree ===' && pstree -p 2>/dev/null | head -20 || ps -ef --forest | head -20

intents.name: network.traceroute
intents.description: Trace the network path to a host
intents.command: traceroute -m 20 {{target}} 2>/dev/null || tracepath {{target}} 2>/dev/null || echo 'traceroute not installed — install with: apt install traceroute'

intents.name: network.dig
intents.description: Detailed DNS query with dig
intents.command: dig {{target}} {{type}} +noall +answer +comments && echo '' && echo '=== Reverse DNS ===' && dig -x {{target}} +short 2>/dev/null || echo '(not an IP)'

intents.name: network.speedtest
intents.description: Test network speed and latency
intents.command: if command -v speedtest-cli &>/dev/null; then speedtest-cli --simple; elif command -v curl &>/dev/null; then echo 'Download speed:' && curl -s -o /dev/null -w 'Speed: %{speed_download} bytes/sec\nTime: %{time_total}s\n' https://speed.cloudflare.com/__down?bytes=10000000 && echo '' && echo 'Latency:' && ping -c 3 1.1.1.1 | tail -1; else echo 'Install speedtest-cli: pip install speedtest-cli'; fi

intents.name: network.connections
intents.description: Show active network connections
intents.command: echo '=== Listening Ports ===' && ss -tlnp 2>/dev/null | head -20 || netstat -tlnp 2>/dev/null | head -20 && echo '' && echo '=== Established Connections ===' && ss -tnp state established 2>/dev/null | head -20 || netstat -tnp 2>/dev/null | grep ESTABLISHED | head -20 && echo '' && echo '=== Connection Count by State ===' && ss -s 2>/dev/null || netstat -s 2>/dev/null | head -15

intents.name: text.count
intents.description: Count lines, words, or characters in a file
intents.command:
echo '=== File Stats ===' && wc -l -w -c {{path}} | awk '{print "Lines: " $1 "
Words: " $2 "
Bytes: " $3 "
File:  " $4}' && echo '' && ls -lh {{path}} | awk '{print "Size: " $5}' && echo '' && echo '=== First 5 lines ===' && head -5 {{path}} && echo '' && echo '=== Last 5 lines ===' && tail -5 {{path}}

intents.name: text.sort
intents.description: Sort a file's contents

intents.name: text.unique
intents.description: Remove duplicate lines from a file
intents.command: echo '=== Unique lines ===' && sort {{path}} | uniq && echo '' && echo '=== Duplicate summary ===' && sort {{path}} | uniq -c | sort -rn | head -10

intents.name: text.diff
intents.description: Compare two files and show differences

intents.name: text.replace
intents.description: Find and replace text in a file
intents.command: echo 'Replacing "{{find}}" with "{{replace}}" in {{path}}' && sed -i 's/{{find}}/{{replace}}/g' {{path}} && echo 'Done. Preview:' && grep -n '{{replace}}' {{path}} | head -5
examples: find and replace foo with bar in settings.json

intents.name: clipboard.copy
intents.description: Copy text or file contents to clipboard
intents.command: if [ -n '{{source}}' ]; then cat {{source}} | (xclip -selection clipboard 2>/dev/null || pbcopy 2>/dev/null || clip.exe 2>/dev/null || echo 'No clipboard tool found'); echo 'Copied to clipboard'; else echo 'Pipe output to: | xclip -sel clip (Linux) or | pbcopy (macOS) or | clip.exe (WSL)'; fi

intents.name: output.save
intents.description: Save command output to a file

intents.name: system.hostname
intents.description: Show or set the system hostname
intents.command: if [ -n '{{name}}' ] && [ '{{name}}' != '{{name}}' ]; then hostnamectl set-hostname {{name}} && echo 'Hostname set to {{name}}'; else hostname && echo '' && hostnamectl 2>/dev/null; fi

intents.name: system.timezone
intents.description: Show or set the system timezone
intents.command: if [ -n '{{tz}}' ] && [ '{{tz}}' != '{{tz}}' ]; then timedatectl set-timezone {{tz}} && echo 'Timezone set to {{tz}}'; else timedatectl 2>/dev/null || (date && cat /etc/timezone 2>/dev/null); fi

intents.name: system.reboot_history
intents.description: Show system reboot history
intents.command: echo '=== Reboot History ===' && last reboot | head -15 && echo '' && echo '=== Last Shutdown ===' && last -x shutdown | head -5 2>/dev/null

intents.name: system.kernel
intents.description: Show kernel and OS information
intents.command: echo '=== Kernel ===' && uname -a && echo '' && echo '=== OS Release ===' && cat /etc/os-release 2>/dev/null && echo '' && echo '=== Hardware ===' && lscpu 2>/dev/null | head -10

intents.name: system.env
intents.description: Show environment variables
intents.command: if [ -n '{{filter}}' ] && [ '{{filter}}' != '{{filter}}' ]; then env | grep -i '{{filter}}' | sort; else env | sort | head -50 && echo '' && echo '(showing first 50 — use filter to narrow)'; fi

intents.name: system.resource_summary
intents.description: Full system resource summary — CPU, memory, disk, load, processes
intents.command: echo '=== System ===' && uname -n && uptime && echo '' && echo '=== CPU ===' && nproc && cat /proc/loadavg && echo '' && echo '=== Memory ===' && free -h && echo '' && echo '=== Disk ===' && df -h --total | tail -5 && echo '' && echo '=== Top Processes ===' && ps aux --sort=-%cpu | head -6 && echo '' && echo '=== Network ===' && ip addr show 2>/dev/null | grep 'inet ' | head -5 || ifconfig 2>/dev/null | grep 'inet ' | head -5

intents.name: network.ip
intents.description: Show IP addresses and network interfaces
intents.command: echo '=== Local IPs ===' && ip addr show 2>/dev/null | grep 'inet ' || ifconfig 2>/dev/null | grep 'inet ' && echo '' && echo '=== Public IP ===' && curl -sf --max-time 3 https://api.ipify.org && echo '' || echo '(could not determine)'

intents.name: network.bandwidth
intents.description: Show network bandwidth usage per interface
intents.command: echo '=== Interface Statistics ===' && cat /proc/net/dev 2>/dev/null | awk 'NR>2{print $1, "RX:", $2, "TX:", $10}' || ifconfig 2>/dev/null && echo '' && echo '=== Active Connections ===' && ss -s 2>/dev/null

intents.name: hardware.info
intents.description: Show hardware information — CPU, RAM, GPU, storage
intents.command: echo '=== CPU ===' && lscpu 2>/dev/null | head -15 || cat /proc/cpuinfo | head -20 && echo '' && echo '=== Memory ===' && free -h && echo '' && echo '=== Storage ===' && lsblk 2>/dev/null | head -15 || fdisk -l 2>/dev/null | head -15 && echo '' && echo '=== GPU ===' && lspci 2>/dev/null | grep -i vga || echo '(no GPU detected)'
examples: how much ram do I have

intents.name: ssh.test
intents.description: Test SSH connectivity to a configured environment

intents.name: docker.exec
intents.description: Run a command inside a Docker container

intents.name: disk.scan
intents.description: Smart scan of all drives with analysis, labels, usage bars, and top space consumers

intents.name: disk.cleanup
intents.description: Scan for reclaimable disk space and clean up with user confirmation
examples: running out of space on c drive

intents.name: db.query
intents.description: Query a database — search, list, count rows in PostgreSQL or MySQL
examples: find sally in the users table
look at my items and find sally

intents.name: db.tables
intents.description: List all tables in a PostgreSQL or MySQL database
examples: what tables are in the database

intents.name: db.describe
intents.description: Describe a table's structure — columns, types, constraints

intents.name: llm.find_claude
intents.description: Find Claude CLI installation and configure notoken to use it

intents.name: package.update
intents.description: Update package lists and upgrade installed packages
intents.command: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get upgrade -y
intents.commandWindows: powershell -Command "Write-Host 'Checking for Windows updates...'; Get-WindowsUpdate -Install -AcceptAll -AutoReboot 2>$null; if($?) { Write-Host 'Updates installed' } else { Write-Host 'Use Settings > Windows Update to check for updates' }"

intents.name: system.health
intents.description: Quick health check — disk, memory, load, and services
intents.command:
echo '=== UPTIME ===' && uptime && echo '
=== MEMORY ===' && free -h && echo '
=== DISK ===' && df -h / && echo '
=== LOAD ===' && cat /proc/loadavg
intents.commandWindows: powershell -Command "Write-Host '=== SYSTEM ==='; systeminfo | Select-String 'OS Name|OS Version|System Boot Time|Total Physical Memory|Available Physical Memory'; Write-Host '\n=== DISK ==='; Get-CimInstance Win32_LogicalDisk -Filter 'DriveType=3' | ForEach-Object { '{0} {1:N1}GB free of {2:N1}GB ({3}%)' -f $_.DeviceID, ($_.FreeSpace/1GB), ($_.Size/1GB), [math]::Round(($_.Size-$_.FreeSpace)/$_.Size*100) }"

intents.name: process.top
intents.description: Show top processes by CPU or memory usage
intents.commandWindows: powershell -Command "Get-Process | Sort-Object -Property CPU -Descending | Select-Object -First 15 Name, Id, @{N='CPU(s)';E={[math]::Round($_.CPU,1)}}, @{N='Mem(MB)';E={[math]::Round($_.WorkingSet64/1MB,0)}} | Format-Table -AutoSize"

intents.name: notoken.rollback
intents.description: Roll back notoken to a previous version
examples: it was working before, go back

intents.name: notoken.versions
intents.description: Show notoken version history and available versions

intents.description: Define a named entity — server, database, or service alias

intents.description: List all defined entities — servers, databases, services
